name: Salt-Rising Bread
recipeIngredient: 3 medium potatoes, pared and cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook potatoes, covered, in 6 cups unsalted water until tender; drain, reserving 5 cups cooking water.
- Use potatoes for another purpose.
2. To make starter:
a. Combine the 1/2 cup flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 cup of the reserved potato water, and yeast.
b. Cover; let stand in warm place several hours.
c. Add the remaining 4 cups potato water and 1/2 cup sugar. Cover; let stand in warm place overnight.
3. Next day:
a. Stir starter; remove 1 cup.
b. Add 2 tablespoons sugar to the 1 cup starter; pour into pint jar. Cover; store in refrigerator until ready to use.
c. To the remaining starter (4 cups), add oil, salt, and enough flour to make a moderately stiff dough.
d. Place in large greased bowl. Let rise in warm place until double, about 1 to 1-1/4 hours.
e. Stir down.
f. Place in greased 12x5-1/2 inch dishpan. Let rise until nearly double, about 1 hour.
g. Bake in 350 degree oven for 60 to 70 minutes.
- Or divide dough into thirds.
h. Place in three greased 9x5x3-inch loaf pans or three greased 1-quart casseroles.
i. Let rise until nearly double.
j. Bake in 375 degree oven for 50 to 55 minutes.
4. To use reserved starter, proceed as before except substitute the 1 cup reserved starter for the 1 cup potato water.
